modules.title: Preparing by removing leaven
modules.exactText:
בִּיעוּרחָמֵץ Elimination of Leaven 1בִּיעוּרחָמֵץ Elimination of Leaven 1בִּיעוּרחָמֵץ Elimination of Leaven 1 Search for Leaven:בְּדִיקַתחָמֵץ i On the night of 14 Nisan, one searches one's property for leaven by candlelight. The search should include all rooms of the house where leavened products may have been brought during the year; it should include all the rooms' corners and the pockets of clothing and pocketbooks. The search need not include rooms where no leaven was ever brought. If the 14th of Nisan is on Shabbat, the search is performed on the Thursday evening before Passover. The blessing is recited and the search is begun immediately afterward. Before the search, it is customary to leave pieces of leaven in rooms that will be searched, so that they will be found and the blessing will not have been in vain.

After the search, the first nullification declaration is recited. The declaration may be recited in Aramaic, or in any other language that the declarer understands. Blessed are You, Adonai bārûk̲ ʾattâ ʾăd̲ōnāyבָּרוּ our God, Sovereign of ʾĕlōhênû melek̲ hāʿôlāmאֱ the universe, who sanc- ʾăšer qiddəšānû bəmiṣ-אֲשֶׁרקִדְּשָׁנוּבְּמִצְוֹתָיו tified us with Your com- wōt̲āyw wəṣiwwānû ʿalוְצִוָּנוּעַלבִּעוּרחָמֵץ mandments and com- biʿûr ḥāmēṣ manded us regard- ing the elimination of leaven. Let any fermentation or kol–ḥămîrāʾ waḥămîʿāʾכׇּל־חֲמִירָאוַחֲמִיעָא leaven in my posession d̲əʾikkāʾ b̲iršût̲î dəlāʾדְאִכָּאבִרְשׁוּתִידְּלָא that I have not noticed ḥămittēh ûd̲əlāʾ baʿărit-חֲמִתֵּהּוּדְלָאבַּעֲרִתֵּהּ and have not eliminated tēh lib̲ṭîl wəlehĕwêלִבְטִילוְלֶהֱוֵיכְּעַפְרָא be nullified and become kəʿap̲rāʾ d̲əʾarʿāʾדְאַרְעָא as the dust of the earth. Elimination of Leaven:בִּיעוּרחָמֵץ ii On the day of the 14th of Nisan ( erev Pesah), before the sixth variable hour, the hametz that was collected in the search the previous day is destroyed (usually by burning). After it is destroyed and before the sixth hour, the following declaration is recited. The declaration may be recited in Aramaic, or in any other language that the declarer understands. iSA OH 444 ;SA OH 431-433 iiSA OH 444 ;SA OH 434

modules.beginnerContext: Explain that searching for and nullifying hametz is a pre-Passover practice, not something guests must improvise at the table. Give a short practical definition of leaven and clearly mark this as optional preparation.

modules.beginnerContext: Introduce the figures as inherited teaching types, not judgments about actual children or guests. Explain the historical language, avoid assigning labels around the table, and give a gentler discussion prompt.
modules.rationale: The two adjacent records preserve the traditional Four Children unit and its responses, stopping at a complete sentence before the source turns to a new scriptural exposition. Keeping the figures together is safer and more intelligible than extracting only the severe description of one child.

modules.title: From ancestry to oppression
modules.exactText:
מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 14מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 14מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 14 “ And Joshua said to wayyōmer yəhôšuaʿוַיֹּ֨אמֶריְה Joshua 24:2-4* all the people, ‘Thus ʾel–kāl–hāʿām kōh–ʾāmarאֶל־כָּל־הָעָ֗םכֹּֽה־אָמַ֣ר said Adonai, the God ʾăd̲ōnāy ʾĕlōhê yiśrāʾēlיְהוָה֮אֱ of Israel: Your an- bəʿēb̲er hannāhārבְּעֵ֣בֶרהַנָּהָ֗ריָשְׁב֤וּ cestors dwelled beyond yāšəb̲û ʾăb̲ôt̲êk̲emאֲבֽ the river from time mēʿôlām teraḥ ʾăb̲îאֲבִ֥יאַבְרָהָ֖םוַאֲבִ֣י immemorial --- Terah, ʾab̲rāhām waʾăb̲î nāḥôrנָח֑ father of Abraham and wayyaʿab̲d̲û ʾĕlōhîmאֲחֵרִֽים father of Nahor --- and ʾăḥērîm they worshipped other gods. I took your father Abra- wāʾeqqaḥ ʾet̲–ʾăb̲îk̲emוָ֠אֶקַּחאֶת־אֲבִיכֶ֤ם ham from the other ʾet̲–ʾab̲rāhām mēʿēb̲erאֶת־אַבְרָהָם֙מֵעֵ֣בֶר side of the river and led hannāhār wāʾôlēk̲ ʾôt̲ôהַנָּהָ֔רוָא him through the whole bək̲āl–ʾereṣ kənāʿan wrb̲בְּכָל־אֶ֣רֶץכְּנָ֑עַן land of Canaan. I mul- wāʾarbeh ʾet̲–zarʿô wāʾet-אֶת־זַרְע֔ aוָאַרְבֶּה֙ tiplied his descendents ten–lô ʾet̲–yiṣḥāqוָֽאֶתֶּן־ל֖ and gave him Isaac. I gave Isaac Jacob and wāʾettēn ləyiṣḥāqוָאֶתֵּ֣ןלְיִצְחָ֔קאֶֽת־יַעֲקֹ֖ב Esau. I gave Esau ʾet̲–yaʿăqōb̲ wəʾet̲–ʿēśāwוְאֶת־עֵשָׂ֑ווָאֶתֵּ֨ןלְעֵשָׂ֜ו Mount Seir to inherit, wāʾettēn ləʿēśāw ʾet̲–harאֶת־הַ֤רשֵׂעִיר֙לָרֶ֣שֶׁת and Jacob and his sons śēʿîr lārešet̲ ʾôt̲ôא went down to Egypt. ” wəyaʿăqōb̲ ûb̲ānāywמִצְרָֽיִם yārəd̲û miṣrāyim Blessed be the One who bārûk̲ šômēr hab̲ṭāḥāt̲ôבָּרוּ keeps His promise to ləyiśrāʾēlלְיִשְׂרָאֵל Israel; blessed be He. bārûk̲ hûʾבָּרוּ For the Holy One šehaqqād̲ôš bārûk̲ hûʾשֶׁהַקָּד blessed be He pre- ḥiššēb̲ ʾet̲ haqqēṣחִשֵּׁבאֶתהַקֵּץ meditated the end [of Israel's enslavement], as He said to our laʿăśôt̲ kəmâ šeʾāmarלַעֲשׂ ancestor Abraham in ləʾab̲rāhām ʾāb̲înû bib̲rît̲לְאַבְרָהָםאָבִינוּ the covenant between bên habbət̲ārîmבִּבְרִיתבֵּיןהַבְּתָרִים the pieces, aכתיב–וארב

מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 15מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 15מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 15 as scripture relates: šenneʾĕmarשֶׁנֶּאֱמַר He said to Abraham, wayyōmer ləʾab̲rāmוַיֹּ֣אמֶרלְאַבְרָ֗םיָדֹ֨עַ Genesis 15:13-14* ‘Know with certainty yād̲ōaʿ tēd̲aʿ kî–g̲ēr yi-תֵּדַ֜עכִּי־גֵ֣ר׀יִהְיֶ֣ה that your descendents hyeh zarʿăk̲ā bəʾereṣ lōʾזַרְעֲ will be strangers in a lāhem waʿăb̲ād̲ûm wəʿin-וַעֲבָד֖וּםוְעִנּ֣וּאֹתָ֑ם land not their own, nû ʾōt̲ām ʾarbaʿ mēʾôt̲אַרְבַּ֥עמֵא֖ and they will serve its šānâ inhabitants, who will afflict them for four hundred years. But I will also judge wəg̲am ʾet̲–haggôy ʾăšerוְגַ֧םאֶת־הַגּ֛ the nation that they yaʿăb̲ōd̲û dān ʾānōk̲îיַעֲבֹ֖דוּדָּ֣ןאָנֹ֑כִי serve, and afterward wəʾaḥărê–k̲ēn yēṣəʾûוְאַחֲרֵי־כֵ֥ןיֵצְא֖וּבִּרְכֻ֥שׁ they will leave with birk̲uš gād̲ôlגָּדֽ great wealth. ” That which stood for wəhîʾ šeʿāməd̲âוְהִיאשֶׁעָמְדָה our ancestors applies to laʾăb̲ôt̲ênû wəlānûלַאֲב us as well.￿ For it was not only one šellōʾ ʾeḥād̲ bilb̲ad̲ ʿāmad̲שֶׁלֹּאאֶחָדבִּלְבַדעָמַד individual who stood up ʿālênû lək̲allôt̲ēnûעָלֵינוּלְכַלּ against us to destroy us. Rather, in every gen- ʾellāʾ šebbək̲ol–dôrאֶלָּאשֶׁבְּכׇל־דּ eration they stand up wād̲ôr ʿôməd̲îm ʿālênûוָד against us to destroy us, lək̲allôt̲ênûלְכַלּ but the Holy One wəhaqqād̲ôš bārûk̲ hûʾוְהַקָּד blessed be He redeems maṣṣîlēnû miyyād̲āmמַצִּילֵנוּמִיָּדָם us from their hands. Go forth and learn ṣēʾ ûləmad̲צֵאוּלְמַד what Laban the mah biqqēš lāb̲ānמַהבִּקֵּשׁלָבָןהָאֲרַמִּי Aramean sought to do hāʾărammî laʿăśôt̲לַעֲשׂ to Jacob, our ancestor. ləyaʿăqōb̲ ʾāb̲înû While Pharaoh only de- šepparʿōh lōʾ g̲āzar ʾellāʾשֶׁפַּרְעֹהלֹאגָזַראֶלָּא creed [death] for the ʿal hazzək̲ārîm wəlāb̲ānעַלהַזְּכָרִיםוְלָבָןבִּקֵּשׁ [Israelite] males, La- biqqēš laʿăqôr ʾet̲ hakkōlלַעֲק ban sought to uproot everything,

מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 16מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 16מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 16 as scripture relates: * Deut. 26:5“ An šenneʾĕmar * dəb̲ārîm k̲whʾărammîאֲרַמִּי֙אֹבֵ֣ד Deuteronomy 26:5*שֶׁנֶּאֱמַר Aramean [Laban] sought ʾōb̲ēd̲ ʾāb̲î wayyēred̲אָבִ֔יוַיֵּ֣ רֶדמִצְרַ֔יְמָה to destroy my father miṣraymâ wayyāg̲or šāmוַיָּ֥ גׇרשָׁ֖םבִּמְתֵ֣ימְעָ֑ט [Jacob]. He [Jacob] went bimt̲ê məʿāṭ wayhî–šāmוַֽיְהִי־שָׁ֕םלְג֥ down to Egypt and ləg̲ôy gād̲ôl ʿāṣûm wārāb̲עָצ֥וּםוָרָֽב sojourned there, few in number, and there he became a great, mighty , and populous nation. ” “He went down to wayyēred̲ miṣraymâוַיֵּ֣ רֶדמִצְרַ֔יְמָה Egypt:” He was compelled by ʾānûs ʿal pî haddibbûrאָנוּסעַלפִּיהַדִּבּוּר the word [of God]. “ And sojourned there:” wayyāg̲or šāmוַיָּ֥ גׇרשָׁ֖ם This teaches that he did məlammēd̲ šellōʾ yārād̲מְלַמֵּדשֶׁלֹּאיָרָד not go down to settle yaʿăqōb̲ ʾāb̲înû ləhiš-יַעֲקֹבאָבִינוּלְהִשְׁתַּקֵּעַ in Egypt, but rather to taqqēaʿ bəmiṣrayim ʾellāʾבְּמִצְרַיִםאֶלָּאלָגוּר sojourn there, lāg̲ûr šāmשָׁם as scripture šenneʾĕmar * bərēšît̲ mzd̲wayyōmərûוַיֹּאמְר֣וּ Genesis 47:4*שֶׁנֶּאֱמַר relates:* Genesis 47:4“They said to ʾel–parʿōh lāg̲ûr bāʾāreṣאֶל־פַּרְעֹ֗הלָג֣וּרבָּאָרֶץ֮ Pharaoh, ‘We have come bānû kî–ʾên mirʿehבָּאנוּ֒כִּי־אֵ֣יןמִרְעֶ֗ה to sojourn in the land, laṣṣōn ʾăšer laʿăb̲ād̲êk̲āלַצֹּאן֙אֲשֶׁ֣רלַעֲבָדֶ֔י for there is no pasture kî–k̲āb̲ēd̲ hārāʿāb̲כִּֽי־כָבֵ֥דהָרָעָ֖בבְּאֶ֣רֶץ for the flocks of your bəʾereṣ kənāʿan wəʿat-כְּנָ֑עַןוְעַתָּ֛היֵֽשְׁבוּ־נָ֥א servants, for the famine tâ yēšəb̲û–nāʾ ʿăb̲ād̲êk̲āעֲבָדֶ֖י is severe in the land of bəʾereṣ gōšen Canaan. Now, therefore, let your servants dwell in the land of Goshen. ’ ”

מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 17מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 17מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 17 “Few in number:” bimt̲ê məʿāṭבִּמְתֵ֣ימְעָ֑ט As kəmâ šenneʾĕmarבְּשִׁבְעִ֣ים Deuteronomy 10:22 *כְּמָהשֶׁנֶּאֱמַר scripture relates: * Deut. 10:22“Your * dəb̲ārîm yk̲b̲bəšib̲ʿîm nep̲eš yārəd̲ûנֶ֔פֶשׁיָרְד֥וּאֲבֹתֶ֖י ancestors went down ʾăb̲ōt̲êk̲ā miṣrāyəmâמִצְרָ֑יְמָהוְעַתָּ֗השָֽׂמְ to Egypt with seventy wəʿattâ śāmək̲ā ʾăd̲ō-יְהוָ֣האֱ people, and now Adonai nāy ʾĕlōhêk̲ā kək̲ôk̲əb̲êהַשָּׁמַ֖יִםלָרֹֽב your God has made you haššāmayim lārōb̲ as numerous as the stars in the heavens. ” “ And there he became a wayhî–šām ləg̲ôy gād̲ôlוַֽיְהִי־שָׁ֕םלְג֥ nation:” This teaches that the məlammēd̲ šehāyûמְלַמֵּדשֶׁהָיוּיִשְׂרָאֵל Israelites were distinct yiśrāʾēl məṣuyyānîmמְצֻיָּנִיםשָׁם there [in Egypt]. šām “Mighty:” ʿāṣûmעָצ֥וּם As kəmâ šenneʾĕmarוּבְנֵ֣י Exodus 1:7*כְּמָהשֶׁנֶּאֱמַר scripture relates: * Exodus 1:7“The * šəmôt̲ ʾzûb̲ənê yiśrāʾēl pārûיִשְׂרָאֵ֗לפָּר֧וּוַֽ יִּשְׁרְצ֛וּ Israelites were fruit- wayyišrəṣû wayyirbûוַיִּרְבּ֥וּוַיַּֽעַצְמ֖וּבִּמְאֹ֣ד ful and swarmed and wayyaʿaṣmû bimʾōd̲מְאֹ֑דוַתִּמָּלֵ֥אהָאָ֖רֶץ multiplied and became məʾōd̲ wattimmālēʾאֹתָֽם exceedingly mighty , and hāʾāreṣ ʾōt̲ām the land was filled with them. ” “ And populous:” wārāb̲וָרָֽב As scripture relates: * Ezekiel 16:7“I kəmâ šenneʾĕmarרְבָבָ֗ה Ezekiel 16:7*כְּמָהשֶׁנֶּאֱמַר have made you abun- * yəḥezqəʾēl ṭzzrəb̲āb̲â kəṣemaḥכְּצֶ֤מַחהַשָּׂדֶה֙נְתַתִּ֔י dant as the growth of haśśād̲eh nət̲attîk̲ wat-וַתִּרְבִּי֙וַֽתִּגְדְּלִ֔יוַתָּבֹ֖אִי the field, and you have tirbî wattig̲dəlî wattāb̲ōʾîבַּעֲדִ֣יעֲדָיִ֑יםשָׁדַ֤יִם become plentiful and baʿăd̲î ʿăd̲āyîm šād̲ayimנָכֹ֨נוּ֙וּשְׂעָרֵ֣ grown and became very nāk̲ōnû ûśəʿārēk̲ ṣim-עֵרֹ֥םוְעֶרְיָֽה beautiful: your breasts mēaḥ wəʾatt ʿērōm formed and your hair wəʿeryâ sprouted, yet you were naked and bare. ”

מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 18מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 18מַגִּיד Discussing the Exodus 18 * Deut. 26:6“ And the Egyptians * dəb̲ārîm k̲wwwayyārēʿû ʾōt̲ānû ham-וַיָּרֵ֧עוּאֹתָ֛נוּהַמִּצְרִ֖ים Deuteronomy 26:6* treated us harshly [or: miṣrîm wayʿannûnûוַיְעַנּ֑וּנוּוַיִּתְּנ֥וּעָלֵ֖ינוּ vilified us] and afflicted wayyittənû ʿālênûעֲבֹדָ֥הקָשָֽׁה us, and they set hard ʿăb̲ōd̲â qāšâ labor upon us. ” “ And the Egyptians vili- wayyārēʿû ʾōt̲ānûוַיָּרֵ֧עוּאֹתָ֛נוּהַמִּצְרִ֖ים fied us:” hammiṣrîm As scripture kəmâ šenneʾĕmarהָ֥בָה Exodus 1:10*כְּמָהשֶׁנֶּאֱמַר relates:* Exodos 1:10[Pharaoh said,] * šəmôt̲ ʾyhāb̲â nit̲ḥakkəmâ lôנִֽתְחַכְּמָ֖הל֑ Come, let us deal pen–yirbeh wəhāyâוְהָיָ֞הכִּֽי־תִקְרֶ֤אנָה wisely with them lest kî–t̲iqrenâ milḥāmâמִלְחָמָה֙וְנ they multiply , so that wənôsap̲ gam–hûʾעַל־שֹׂ֣נְאֵ֔ינוּוְנִלְחַם־בָּ֖נוּ when war comes they ʿal–śōnəʾênû wənil-וְעָלָ֥המִן־הָאָֽרֶץ join our enemies and ḥam–bānû wəʿālâ fight against us and go min–hāʾāreṣ up out of the land. ” “ And afflicted us:” wayʿannûnûוַיְעַנּ֑וּנוּ As kəmâ šenneʾĕmarוַיָּשִׂ֤ימוּ Exodus 1:11*כְּמָהשֶׁנֶּאֱמַר scripture relates: * Exodus 1:11“They * šəmôt̲ ʾywayyāśîmû ʿālāyw śārêעָלָיו֙שָׂרֵ֣ימִסִּ֔יםלְמַ֥עַן placed taskmasters over missîm ləmaʿan ʿan-עַנֹּת֖ them in order to af- nōt̲ô bəsib̲lōt̲ām wayy-עָרֵ֤ימִסְכְּנ flict them with their ib̲en ʿārê miskənôt̲אֶת־פִּתֹ֖םוְאֶת־רַעַמְסֵֽס burdens, and they ləp̲arʿōh ʾet̲–pit̲ōm built storage cities wəʾet̲–raʿamsēs for Pharaoh, [called] Pithom and Ramses. ” “ And they set hard wayyittənû ʿālênûוַיִּתְּנ֥וּעָלֵ֖ינוּעֲבֹדָ֥ה labor upon us:” ʿăb̲ōd̲â qāšâקָשָֽׁה As scripture kəmâ šenneʾĕmarוַיַּעֲבִ֧דוּ Exodus 1:13*כְּמָהשֶׁנֶּאֱמַר relates:* Exodus 1:13“The Egyptians * šəmôt̲ ʾyg̲wayyaʿăb̲id̲û miṣrayimמִצְרַ֛יִםאֶת־בְּנֵ֥ייִשְׂרָאֵ֖ל worked the Israelites ʾet̲–bənê yiśrāʾēl bəp̲ārek̲בְּפָֽרֶ severely . ”

modules.beginnerContext: State the narrative arc before the detailed verses: the family went to Egypt, became a people, and was enslaved and harmed. Explain that the people's cry and liberation continue in the following part of Maggid. Introduce each scriptural quotation with one sentence of plain-language meaning.
modules.rationale: Five source-order segments preserve the traditional verse-by-verse movement from ancestry through oppression and stop after a complete scriptural sentence. The later page records were removed because their extracted text ends mid-exposition.

And I will ex- ʾeʿĕśeh šəp̲āṭîm ʾănîיְהוָֽה ecute judgment against ʾăd̲ōnāy all the gods of Egypt. modules.beginnerContext: Explain that the plagues are catastrophes within the liberation story, name the cup-diminishing custom, and say explicitly that the table does not celebrate civilian suffering. Use wine or grape juice and give one action cue before the list.
modules.rationale: The adjacent passages retain the narrative and ritual treatment of the plagues while stopping before the most elaborate numerical multiplication, making a more usable adult or mixed-age unit.

modules.beginnerContext: Introduce Dayenu as a cumulative gratitude song, then point to the traditional symbols and explain each one before the every-generation teaching. Signal the transition from telling into praise.
modules.rationale: Seven consecutive passages preserve the source's complete theological and ritual turn from gratitude through Pesach, matzah, maror, lived memory, and Hallel.

modules.beginnerContext: Give practical instructions before the liturgy: wash hands individually or by pouring for one another, eat matzah, then bitter herb with haroset and the sandwich. Define haroset and provide allergy-safe substitutions.
modules.rationale: The three adjacent source records hold the table rituals in their proper order and can serve as a concise traditional block when paired with accessible host directions.

For everything, Adonai wəʿal hakkōl ʾăd̲ō-וְעַלהַכֹּליְהוָה our God, we thank nāy ʾĕlōhênû ʾănaḥnûאֱ You and bless You. môd̲îm lāk̲ ûməb̲ārək̲îmלָ May Your name be ʾôt̲āk̲ yit̲bārak̲ šimk̲āיִתְבָּרַ blessed in the mouth bəp̲î kol–ḥay tāmîd̲כׇּל־חַיתָּמִידלְע of every living being ləʿôlām wāʿed̲וָעֶד always, forever and ever! As scripture says: * Deut. 8:10“You kakkāt̲ûb̲ * dəb̲ārîm ḥywəʾāk̲altāוְאָכַלְתָּ֖ Deuteronomy 8:10*כַּכָּתוּב will eat and be satisfied, wəśāb̲āʿətā ûb̲ērak̲tāוְשָׂבָ֑עְתָּוּבֵֽרַכְתָּ֙ and you will bless ʾet̲–ʾăd̲ōnāy ʾĕlōhêk̲āאֶת־יְהוָ֣האֱ Adonai your God for the ʿal–hāʾāreṣ haṭṭōb̲â ʾăšerעַל־הָאָ֥רֶץהַטֹּבָ֖האֲשֶׁ֥ר good land that He gave nāt̲an–lāk̲נָֽתַן־לָֽ you. ”

Please, do nāʾ ʾal–taṣrîk̲ēnû ʾăd̲ō-מַתְּנַתבָּשָׂרוָדָםוְלֹא not make us dependent nāy ʾĕlōhênû lōʾ lîd̲êלִידֵיהַלְוָאָתָםכִּי on the charity of flesh mattənat̲ bāśār wād̲āmאִםלְיָדְ and blood or on their wəlōʾ lîd̲ê halwāʾāt̲ām kîהַפְּתוּחָההַקְּד loans. modules.beginnerContext: Explain that Barech is the gratitude said after eating and tell guests whether the leader will read or everyone will respond. Offer a shortened path without implying that a mixed-belief guest must affirm every theological line.
modules.rationale: The four source-order records provide a coherent entrance into grace and communal response, retaining the liturgical progression instead of using a blessing fragment out of context.
